blurb: Supports B.C.'s farmers, ranchers and food producers — farm business supports, food security, agricultural land use, and the growth of sustainable local food systems. Also responsible for liquor and cannabis regulation since July 2025.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Build and maintain a competitive and profitable agriculture and food sector
goals.description: The ministry helps farm and food businesses start, grow, manage income risk, and reach new domestic and export markets, and connects British Columbians with locally grown food.

main_services.name: Grow and market a farm or food business

supporting.name: Get advice and connect with ministry agriculture programs
supporting.desc: AgriService BC connects farm and food businesses to ministry programs, regional advisors, funding, and events across B.C.

supporting.name: Apply for farm business planning and development funding
supporting.desc: Advisory and funding support (including the B.C. Agri-Business Planning Program and New Entrant Farm Business Accelerator Program) helps new and existing farm businesses plan, start up and grow.
supporting.users: Farm businesses, new entrants to agriculture

supporting.name: Find land to lease or rent for farming
supporting.desc: The B.C. Land Matching Program connects new and expanding farmers with landholders who have land available for agricultural use.
supporting.users: New entrants and expanding farm businesses

supporting.name: Expand into new domestic and export markets for your products
supporting.desc: Buy BC, Feed BC and related trade and market development programs help producers and processors grow domestic sales and diversify export markets, including in response to U.S. tariff threats.
supporting.users: Agriculture, seafood and food and beverage businesses

channels.label: Feed BC and the B.C. Food Hub Network

supporting.name: Access agritech and innovation support for your farm
supporting.desc: The Agritech Concierge service connects producers with technology, research and innovation resources to modernize farm operations.
supporting.users: Farm and food businesses adopting new technology

main_services.name: Get agriculture insurance and income protection

supporting.name: Enrol in AgriStability to stabilize your farm income
supporting.desc: AgriStability provides payments when a farm's net income drops by 30% or more, covering $0.80 on every dollar lost.
supporting.users: Farmers and ranchers of all types and sizes

supporting.name: Get production insurance for crop losses
supporting.desc: Crop-based Production Insurance protects farm income against yield reductions from weather and disaster events, with premiums up to 80% government-subsidized.

supporting.name: Get compensation for crop or livestock losses caused by wildlife
supporting.desc: The Agriculture Wildlife Program compensates producers for crop and livestock losses caused by wildlife.

supporting.name: Apply for emergency flood or disaster recovery funding for your farm
supporting.desc: One-time recovery funding, such as the B.C. 2026 Food Security Flood Recovery Program for Sumas Prairie producers, helps cover uninsurable infrastructure repair and return-to-production costs after a disaster.
supporting.users: Livestock and crop producers affected by disasters

channels.label: B.C. 2026 Food Security Flood Recovery Program

relationships.service: Apply for disaster financial assistance for your small business or farm

main_services.name: Buy and learn about local food

supporting.name: Find B.C.-grown food, farmers markets and local seafood
supporting.desc: The Buy BC program helps consumers discover the variety of B.C.-grown food and beverages and the producers behind them.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: B.C.'s agriculture and food sector manages risk and adapts to change
goals.description: The ministry safeguards animal and plant health, food safety and agricultural land, and helps the sector prepare for market uncertainty, climate impacts, pests and disease.

main_services.name: Keep livestock, crops and food safe

supporting.name: Register your farm's premises for emergency and disease traceability
supporting.desc: Premises ID links livestock and poultry operations to their geographic locations to support emergency planning and disease response.

supporting.name: Report and manage animal disease and health concerns
supporting.desc: The Office of the Chief Veterinarian and the Animal Health Centre diagnostic laboratory monitor and respond to livestock and poultry disease, including avian influenza.

legislation.full_name: Animal Health Act  -  Disease control and inspection powers

supporting.name: Get a licence to operate a dairy, poultry, fur or game farming operation
supporting.desc: Provincial licences are required for dairy, fur farming, game farming and poultry operations; dairy licensing and standards flow from the Milk Industry Act.
supporting.users: Dairy, poultry, fur and game farm operators

legislation.full_name: Milk Industry Act  -  Dairy plant licensing and standards

supporting.name: Get a meat inspection licence to operate a slaughter establishment
supporting.desc: A graduated licensing approach under the Food Safety Act's Meat Inspection Regulation regulates slaughter establishments in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Food Safety Act  -  Meat Inspection Regulation (slaughter establishment licensing)

supporting.name: Develop a food safety plan for your processing business
supporting.desc: Food processing businesses must implement formal, documented food safety and sanitation plans under the Food Safety Act.

legislation.full_name: Food Safety Act  -  Duty to ensure food safety and food safety plans

supporting.name: Get help identifying and managing crop pests and plant disease
supporting.desc: The Plant Health Laboratory and related resources help growers identify and manage insects, plant diseases and weeds affecting agricultural crops.

supporting.name: Register your beehives
supporting.desc: Beekeepers must register their apiaries under the Bee Act to support disease surveillance and control.

legislation.full_name: Bee Act  -  Registration of apiaries and beekeepers

main_services.name: Resolve a farm practices or land use concern

supporting.name: Resolve a complaint about noise, odour or dust from a farm operation
supporting.desc: The BC Farm Industry Review Board hears complaints about nuisances from farm operations and determines whether they result from a normal farm practice, protected under the Farm Practices Protection (Right to Farm) Act.

legislation.full_name: Farm Practices Protection (Right to Farm) Act  -  Determination of normal farm practice

supporting.name: Protect farmland through the Agricultural Land Reserve
supporting.desc: The Agricultural Land Reserve is a provincial land use designation, administered by the Agricultural Land Commission, that recognizes farming as the priority use and restricts non-agricultural uses of the land base.

legislation.full_name: Agricultural Land Commission Act  -  Administration of the Agricultural Land Reserve

relationships.service: Get help adding tourism experiences to your farm

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Fish and seafood are farmed, harvested and processed sustainably
goals.description: The ministry licenses and supports B.C.'s aquaculture operations, commercial fisheries, and seafood processing sector.

supporting.name: Get a licence to farm fish, shellfish or aquatic plants
supporting.desc: Aquaculture operators need site tenure and licensing to culture salmon and other finfish, shellfish, or marine plants in B.C.'s coastal waters.

supporting.name: Get a seafood processor licence
supporting.desc: A seafood processor licence is required to operate a seafood processing or cold-storage facility under the Fish and Seafood Act.

channels.label: FrontCounter BC or Service BC location

legislation.full_name: Fish and Seafood Act  -  Licensing (seafood processors)

supporting.name: Get a fish receiver licence
supporting.desc: A fish receiver licence is required for a shore-based facility, vehicle or vessel that receives fish directly from a commercial fisher, under the Fish and Seafood Act.

channels.label: FrontCounter BC or Service BC location

legislation.full_name: Fish and Seafood Act  -  Licensing (fish receivers)

supporting.name: Get a fisher vendor licence to sell your catch directly
supporting.desc: Commercial fishers who sell their catch directly to the public, restaurants or retail stores need a fisher vendor licence under the Fish and Seafood Act.

channels.label: FrontCounter BC or Service BC location

legislation.full_name: Fish and Seafood Act  -  Licensing (fisher vendors)

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Modernize B.C.'s liquor and cannabis licensing and enforcement system
goals.description: Since assuming responsibility for the Liquor Distribution Branch and Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch in July 2025, the ministry is simplifying and expediting liquor and cannabis licensing while focusing enforcement on high-risk, repeat non-compliant businesses.

main_services.name: Get a liquor or cannabis licence

supporting.name: Apply for a liquor licence or permit for your business or event
supporting.desc: The Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch licenses restaurants, bars, pubs, liquor manufacturers, private liquor stores, caterers and special events under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act.

legislation.full_name: Liquor Control and Licensing Act  -  Licensing of establishments and events

supporting.name: Apply for a cannabis retail or production licence
supporting.desc: The Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch licenses private cannabis stores and other cannabis activities under the Cannabis Control and Licensing Act.

legislation.full_name: Cannabis Control and Licensing Act  -  Licensing of cannabis retail and related activities

supporting.name: Get certified to serve liquor or sell cannabis responsibly
supporting.desc: Serving It Right and Selling It Right are mandatory responsible-service certification courses for people serving liquor or selling cannabis.

main_services.name: Comply with liquor and cannabis rules

supporting.name: Report a liquor or cannabis compliance concern
supporting.desc: The public can submit complaints about licensed establishments, which the Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch investigates and enforces under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act and Cannabis Control and Licensing Act.
supporting.users: General public, neighbours of licensed establishments

legislation.full_name: Liquor Control and Licensing Act  -  Compliance and enforcement
Cannabis Control and Licensing Act  -  Compliance and enforcement

foundational_services.name: Agricultural land use policy and mapping
foundational_services.desc: Policy, land use inventories, and mapping work supporting agricultural land protection and use planning.

foundational_services.name: Animal and plant health laboratory diagnostics
foundational_services.desc: Diagnostic laboratory services (Animal Health Centre, Plant Health Laboratory) supporting animal and plant disease surveillance underlying public-facing programs.

foundational_services.name: Agriculture and seafood statistics and market intelligence
foundational_services.desc: Collection and publication of industry performance, market and trade statistics for the agriculture and seafood sector.
